(Spanish pronunciation: [[ˈsintja roˈðɾiɣes]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish"); born May 8, 1984, in [Monclova, Coahuila](/wiki/Monclova,_Coahuila "Monclova, Coahuila"), Mexico) is a Mexican [singer](/wiki/Vocalist "Vocalist") and [reality television personality](/wiki/Reality_television "Reality television").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

In this [Spanish name](/wiki/Spanish_name "Spanish name"), the first or paternal [surname](/wiki/Surname "Surname") is  *Rodríguez* and the second or maternal family name is  *Ruiz*.

| Cynthia Rodríguez | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Born | Cynthia Deyanira Rodríguez Ruiz   (1984-05-08) May 8, 1984 (age 41)  [Monclova, Coahuila](/wiki/Monclova,_Coahuila "Monclova, Coahuila"), [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") |
| **Musical career** | |
| Genres | [Pop](/wiki/Pop_music "Pop music"), [dance](/wiki/Dance_music "Dance music") |
| Years active | 2005–present |
| Label | [Warner Music](/wiki/Warner_Music "Warner Music") |
| Website | [Official website](http://www.cynthiaoficial.com) |
| Musical artist | |

**Cynthia Deyanira Rodríguez Ruiz** (Spanish pronunciation: [[ˈsintja roˈðɾiɣes]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish"); born May 8, 1984, in [Monclova, Coahuila](/wiki/Monclova,_Coahuila "Monclova, Coahuila"), Mexico) is a Mexican [singer](/wiki/Vocalist "Vocalist") and [reality television personality](/wiki/Reality_television "Reality television").

She was a contestant on the [reality TV](/wiki/Reality_TV "Reality TV") show *[La Academia](/wiki/La_Academia "La Academia")*, coming in fourth place. Rodríguez later competed on the reality show *[Desafio de Estrellas](/wiki/Desafio_de_Estrellas "Desafio de Estrellas")*. She was voted off the show right before the finals, with only five other contestants left. Her album *[Soy](/wiki/Soy_(Cynthia_album) "Soy (Cynthia album)")* was released in June 2006, just as she left *Desafio de Estrellas*. She was selected to sing the theme song for a television series on [TV Azteca](/wiki/TV_Azteca "TV Azteca"), called *Looking for a Man*.

Cynthia later played an important antagonist role in a Mexican "[telenovela](/wiki/Telenovela "Telenovela")" called *Bellezas Indomables*. Concurrently, her second album *Provocame* was released in March 2008.

In the present, Cynthia is playing the role of the protagonist's best friend in the "[telenovela](/wiki/Telenovela "Telenovela")" *Mujer Comprada*, which airs daily on [TV Azteca](/wiki/TV_Azteca "TV Azteca"). At the same time, she is the hostess of a music videos show called *Festival Azteca Music*, which airs weekly on [Azteca America](/wiki/Azteca_America "Azteca America"). She is also planning the release of her third album for 2010.

In 2011, after retiring from the Warner Music Mexico label after three years, Cynthia signed a contract with the record label Sony Music, and recorded her third album Tú como yo, where she ventured into [regional Mexican music](/wiki/Regional_Mexican_music "Regional Mexican music") ([grupera](/wiki/Grupera "Grupera")) with songs written and composed by various singer-songwriters and composers such as [Espinoza Paz](/wiki/Espinoza_Paz "Espinoza Paz"), [Áureo Baqueiro](/w/index.php?title=%C3%81ureo_Baqueiro&action=edit&redlink=1 "Áureo Baqueiro (page does not exist)"), [A.B. Quintanilla](/wiki/A.B._Quintanilla "A.B. Quintanilla"), [Leonel García](/wiki/Leonel_Garc%C3%ADa "Leonel García") (ex [Sin Bandera](/wiki/Sin_Bandera "Sin Bandera")) and [Paty Cantú](/wiki/Paty_Cant%C3%BA "Paty Cantú"), consolidating her musical career and positioning within the Top 10 on various radio stations in Mexico, and with which she is nicknamed "La princesa grupera". It includes the songs "De ti", "Ven", "Enamorada" and the eponymous song "Tú como yo", which is a duet with rapper and former member of the Mexican group [Kumbia All Starz](/wiki/Kumbia_All_Starz "Kumbia All Starz"), [Ricky Rick](/w/index.php?title=Ricky_Rick&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ricky Rick (page does not exist)"); In addition to the theme song for the novela [Huérfanas](/wiki/Hu%C3%A9rfanas "Huérfanas"), "Cuando no estás," a concert tour was also held in both the United States and Mexico to promote this album.

That same year, she continued working at [TV Azteca](/wiki/TV_Azteca "TV Azteca"), this time as a co-host of the support program [Red Ángel](/w/index.php?title=Red_%C3%81ngel&action=edit&redlink=1 "Red Ángel (page does not exist)"), alongside actor and host [Omar Fierro](/wiki/Omar_Fierro "Omar Fierro").

Years later, in 2013, she participated in the telenovelas [Los Rey](/wiki/Los_Rey "Los Rey") and [Corazón en condominio](/wiki/Coraz%C3%B3n_en_condominio "Corazón en condominio"), starring in the latter alongside [Víctor García](/wiki/V%C3%ADctor_Garc%C3%ADa_(Mexican_singer) "Víctor García (Mexican singer)"), with whom she also performed the novela's theme song. In 2014, she appeared as a judge on the impersonation program [Soy tu doble](/w/index.php?title=Soy_tu_doble&action=edit&redlink=1 "Soy tu doble (page does not exist)") (I Am Your Double). Later, producer Eduardo Paz announced her starring role in the musical ¡Qué rico mambo!, making her debut as a theater actress.

In 2015, Cynthia, along with Víctor García and Alex Garza, hosted the entertainment program Viva el Show for TV Azteca.

In 2017, she released her single "Conquistame" (Conquistame), hosted the grupera music program [Corazon grupero](/w/index.php?title=Corazon_grupero&action=edit&redlink=1 "Corazon grupero (page does not exist)"), and has also appeared in the film "El Hubiera Sí Existe" and the telenovelas "Las Malcriadas" and "Educando a Nina" (Educating Nina). The latter is a Mexican version of the Argentine series of the same name, in which she plays the lead role of twins. She also recorded a duet with actress and singer Mariana Seoane, a cover of the song "Me Equivoqué" (Me Equivoqué).

In 2018, Cynthia returned to the reality show [La Academia](/wiki/La_Academia "La Academia"), this time as a backstage presenter and co-presenter; and months later, she joined the animation of the miscellaneous program Todo un show, replacing the presenter [Anette Cuburu](/w/index.php?title=Anette_Cuburu&action=edit&redlink=1 "Anette Cuburu (page does not exist)"), Cynthia shared with the YouTuber, actor and television presenter [Roger González](/w/index.php?title=Roger_Gonz%C3%A1lez&action=edit&redlink=1 "Roger González (page does not exist)"), the entertainment journalist [Laura G](/wiki/Laura_G "Laura G") and the remembered Argentine actor and host [Fernando del Solar](/w/index.php?title=Fernando_del_Solar&action=edit&redlink=1 "Fernando del Solar (page does not exist)"), to later join her colleagues as the main cast of the [morning show](/wiki/Morning_show "Morning show") [Venga la alegría](/wiki/Venga_la_alegr%C3%ADa "Venga la alegría") after the early end of Todo un show; she remained at VLA for three years.

At the beginning of 2021, in April, Cynthia Rodríguez launched her cosmetics brand, CYN: by Cynthia Rodríguez, where she manages her products.

After more than 15 years in television, in 2022, Cynthia left TV Azteca and the morning show VLA to begin pursuing several personal commitments for a long period away from the media.

Additionally, Cynthia launched her official [YouTube channel](/wiki/YouTube_channel "YouTube channel"), CynthiaTV, where she hosts various segments related to beauty, healthy recipes, and fitness. She also does interviews with various artists, challenges, and reminisces about her music videos. Currently, this channel has more than 920,000 subscribers.
